front: Poem: narrator asks 'Is there balm in Gilead?' to a creature perched 'upon a bust of Pallas' that repeats 'Nevermore'
work: Poetry (The Raven, Annabel Lee, To Helen, etc.)

front: Poem: speaker compares beauty to 'Nicean barks of yore' bringing him 'To the glory that was Greece / And the grandeur that was Rome'
work: Poetry (The Raven, Annabel Lee, To Helen, etc.)

front: Poem: 'Death has reared himself a throne / In a strange city lying alone'; contains a 'wilderness of glass'
back: The City in the Sea (Edgar Allan Poe)
work: Poetry (The Raven, Annabel Lee, To Helen, etc.)

front: Poem: 'vast formless things' with 'Condor wings' attack a stage; 'the play is the tragedy, Man'
work: Poetry (The Raven, Annabel Lee, To Helen, etc.)

front: Short Story: narrator reads The Mad Trist aloud to a man increasingly disturbed by sounds; twin sister Madeline appears in doorway; house slides into a tarn
back: The Fall of the House of Usher (Edgar Allan Poe)

front: Short Story: narrator obsesses over old man's pale blue 'vulture eye'; hides body under floorboards; asks 'why will you say I am mad?'
Short Story: William Legrand finds a scarabaeus with a death's-head; 'Bishop's Hostel' decoded as Bessop's Castle; popularized cryptography
Author: essay declares 'the death of a beautiful woman is unquestionably the most poetical topic in the world' while explaining the composition of 'The Raven'
Author: Baudelaire spent decades translating this writer into French; Mallarme wrote a poem about this author's 'Tomb'
Short Story: cat Pluto's image appears on a wall with a hangman's noose; narrator murders wife with an axe while trying to kill the second title animal
